histogram_plot.caption: Histogram of number_of_device_registered across 5,630 customers. Orange = mean, purple = median. The gap between them confirms skew direction: mean > median ⇒ right-skewed, mean < median ⇒ left-skewed.

boxplot.caption: Box plot reads as: box = 25th–75th percentile (the middle 50%), line inside box = median, whiskers extend to 1.5 × IQR, dots beyond = outliers. Large vertical separation between the two medians = strong churn predictor. Box overlap that is still high means the feature alone does NOT distinguish the two groups.

mann_whitney_u.narration: Mann-Whitney U = 1,891,637. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature.

cohens_d.narration: Cohen's d = +0.29 — small effect size. Values are higher in churners (positive shift).

outliers.narration: 397 outliers by the IQR rule (7.1% of rows). p99 = 6.00, max = 6.00. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

narrative.distribution: Distribution is approximately symmetric (skewness = -0.40). Mean (3.69) < median (4.00) — confirms the left skew.
narrative.boxplot: Churned median (4.00) is 0.00 lower than retained median (4.00). A weak separator — some shift but substantial overlap between groups.
narrative.separation: Mann-Whitney U = 1,891,637. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature. Cohen's d = +0.29 — small effect size. Values are higher in churners (positive shift).
narrative.outliers: 397 outliers by the IQR rule (7.1% of rows). p99 = 6.00, max = 6.00. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

recommendation.transform_reason: distribution close to symmetric OR outlier mass minimal
recommendation.outlier_action: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2
recommendation.rationale: Signal: weak (|Cohen's d| = 0.29, p = 3.054e-14). Transform: none — distribution close to symmetric OR outlier mass minimal. Outlier handling: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2.
